steps.title: Spot the contrast between appearance and expert knowledge
steps.content: The first sentence states the volcanoes “may look similar from afar,” but the second sentence reveals that volcanologists can distinguish them by formation. The transition word must bridge this contradiction between visual similarity and expert differentiation.

steps.title: Match the contrast signal
steps.content:
”Though” is a concessive word meaning “nevertheless”—it directly signals that despite the visual likeness, experts distinguish them by formation.
The other three are additive (“for example,” “in addition”) or causal (“therefore”), none of which express contrast. This matches choice D — D is correct.
